LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT GRAMERCY TOWERS CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES IS GRANTED IN PART. GTCA IS AWARDED $10,000 FOR THE FEES THAT ARE "REASONABLY ALLOCABLE TO ACHIEVING" ITS "VICTORY" IN STRIKING THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION. (AREA 51 PRODUCTIONS, INC. V. CITY OF ALAMEDA (2018) 20 CAL. APP. 5TH 581, 605). BASED ON THE COURT'S RECALL OF THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION AND REVIEW OF THE PAPERS FILED ON THIS MOTION, $10,000 IS A REASONABLE AMOUNT OF FEES THAT WERE INCURRED BY GTCA ON THE SOLE CAUSE OF ACTION IT PREVAILED ON ITS ANTI-SLAPP MOTION AND FOR SEEKING FEES FOR THAT LIMITED VICTORY. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M.GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, TORREANOM@AOL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/HK)

Notice Of Application And Application For An Order To Show Cause Regarding Why The Court Should Not Find Contempt And Order Sanctions For Violations Of The Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Jun-06-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. David J. Millstein of Millstein & Associates, Tel. No. 1-415-348-0348 appearing
for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Cang Le of Adams Sterling, Tel. No. 1-951-227-9167 appearing for the Defendant.


RULING - A R G U E D;The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Kazuko Artus' application for an order to show cause why the court should not find contempt and order sanctions for violations of the preliminary injunction is denied. Dr. Artus has not shown that defendant Gramercy Towers Condominium Association willfully disobeyed the preliminary injunction order filed February 2, 2018. No election was held on January 30, 2018. GTCA appointed three inspectors for the February 6, 2018 election and all of them signed statements of independence and none of them have been shown to be biased in any way. While there is a plausible argument that the participation of Condominium Financial Management and persons affiliated with it in the February 6, 2018 was a technical violation of Civil Code 5110(b), there is no indication in the record that GTCA avoided the requirements of the February 2 order by retaining CFM for the February 6 election. It is especially noteworthy that Dr. Artus does not contend that the there was any irregularity in the voting or counting of ballots for the February 6 election. Mr. Adams to prepare for of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR # 8600 =302/hk

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Conduct Specified Discovery Pending Defendant'S Motion To Strike Pursuant To Code Civ. Proc. 425.16(G) ("Anti-Slapp")
Off Calendar Mar-01-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Cang Le, Esq. 800-464-2817 of Adams Stirling for Defendant GRAMERCY TOWERS CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Discussed. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings adopts the tenatative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF KAZUKO ARTUS Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Conduct Specified Discovery Pending Defendant'S Motion To Strike Pursuant To Code Civ. Proc. 425.16(G) ("Anti-Slapp")

Plaintiff Kazuko Artus' motion for leave to conduct specified discovery pending defendant Gramercy Towers Condominium Association's anti-SLAPP motion to strike is denied. Dr. Artus fails to show she needs any of the discovery she wishes to propound to enable her to defeat GTCA's anti-SLAPP motion. Moreover, Dr. Artus provides no explanation why the motion was not filed earlier. The motion appears to be an arguably impermissible "Plan C" approach predicated on Dr. Artus' view that the discovery she seeks is only needed in the event the court (erroneously in her view) rejects her first and second prong arguments in her opposition papers to the anti-SLAPP motion. Because resolution of this motion affects the resolution of the anti-SLAPP motion and the demurrer, this motion will be decided prior to those other motions.

Counsel for GTCA is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)
